items.title: Denmark midfielder Christian Eriksen collapses on pitch during international friendly
items.summary: Match against Ukraine abandoned after incident Danish FA say Eriksen ‘conscious’ after treatment Denmark’s former Manchester United and Tottenham midfielder Christian Eriksen collapsed on the pitch in a match against Ukraine on Sunday, but was conscious as he was taken from the field by medics. items.title: Eriksen conscious after collapsing during Denmark friendly
items.summary: Christian Eriksen is conscious after collapsing during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine.

items.title: Zverev wins French Open title after dramatic five-set win against Cobolli
items.summary: Alexander Zverev finally claimed an elusive Grand Slam title in his fourth final as he defeated 14th-ranked Flavio Cobolli in a dramatic French Open climax.

items.title: Alpine request review of Monaco pit lane penalties as Gasly left 'heartbroken'
items.summary: Alpine have requested a right of review from the FIA for penalties applied for pit lane speeding during the Monaco Grand Prix after their driver Pierre Gasly was demoted from a podium finish.

items.title: Eriksen conscious after collapsing in Denmark game
items.summary: Christian Eriksen collapsed during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine but is now conscious, says the Danish Football Association.

items.title: Christian Eriksen’s internal defibrillator saved his life after collapsing in Denmark friendly, says team doctor
items.summary: CHRISTIAN ERIKSEN'S defibrillator saved his life after he collapsed again during Denmark's friendly with Ukraine. items.title: Footballer Fatjon Bunjaku, 22, dead in serious car crash as Kosovo national team pay emotional tribute to rising star
items.summary: A RISING footballer has died in a serious car crash, with the Kosovo national team paying an emotional tribute to the future star. items.title: Denmark: Eriksen 'conscious' after on-pitch collap...
items.summary: Christian Eriksen is "conscious and doing well under the circumstances" after collapsing during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine, which forced the game to be abandoned.

items.title: Leão sent off for punching opponent in Portugal wi...
items.summary: Portugal forward Rafael Leão was sent off for punching an opponent during a 2-0 win over Chile in a World Cup warmup on Saturday.

items.title: Dembélé: France to end Deschamps' reign on top
items.summary: France winger Ousmane Dembele said the impending departure of manager Didier Deschamps is not a distraction for the squad heading into the FIFA World Cup, but is more of a motivator for the team to finish his reign on a high note.

items.title: Kane: Heat 'won't be a factor' at World Cup
items.summary: Harry Kane has said he thinks England's World Cup preparations means the much-publicised high temperatures at this summer's tournament "won't be a factor."

items.title: England win over New Zealand provides reminder of ...
items.summary: England's victory on Saturday proved one thing: Harry Kane is pivotal to his team's hopes of World Cup glory.

items.title: Utd target Éderson called up to Brazil WC squad
items.summary: Atalanta midfielder and Manchester United transfer target Éderson has been called up to Brazil's World Cup squad following an injury to Wesley.

items.title: Transfer rumors, news: UCL finalists Arsenal, PSG ...
items.summary: Arsenal could face strong competition from Paris Saint-Germain for the signature of Nottingham Forest midfielder Morgan Gibbs-White. Transfer Talk has the latest.

items.title: Iran player criticizes FIFA as team lands in Mexic...
items.summary: Iran defender Ehsan Hajsafi has criticised FIFA after the team arrived in Mexico on Sunday with some members of their entourage still lacking U.S. visas.

items.title: Poch finally feels WC 'excitement' building in U.S...
items.summary: United States coach Mauricio Pochettino said he could finally feel the excitement he expected to see from the American fanbase during his team's 2-1 loss to Germany on Saturday at Soldier Field -- its final game before the start of the World Cup.

items.title: 👀Who could use World Cup to get big transfer?
items.summary: The World Cup is the biggest stage in sports, so which players are hoping to use it to get their next big transfer?

items.title: Slow starts aside, Socceroos are full of promise
items.summary: A week from their opener vs. Türkiye, the Socceroos will take confidence from a 1-1 draw with Switzerland.

items.title: Kit ranking: All 105 jerseys on show
items.summary: The 2026 World Cup will be the first with 48 teams, bringing a total of more than 100 kits!
